steps.title: Identify the sentence structure required
steps.content: The sentence begins: “The reed of a wind instrument is the mouthpiece ______” The blank is completed by a relative clause introduced by where that modifies “mouthpiece.” A clause embedded in a declarative sentence uses normal subject-verb word order and ends with a period, not a question mark.

steps.title: Test each choice for word order and end punctuation
steps.content:
• Choice A — where sound is made?: Word order is correct (subject sound before verb is made), but the question mark turns a declarative sentence into a question. ✗
  • Choice B — where is sound made.: Word order is inverted (verb is before subject sound), producing interrogative structure inside a declarative sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — where sound is made.: Normal subject-verb order and a period—correct declarative clause, correct end punctuation. ✓
  • Choice D — where is sound made?: Both flaws combined—inverted word order and a question mark. ✗
C is correct.
